title: rubus tereticaulis var. subserpens
intro: Rubus tereticaulis var. subserpens, also known as a variant of the western thimbleberry, is a type of plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus tereticaulis var. subserpens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and provide antioxidant effects. The plant is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, taken as capsules, or applied topically to the skin.
